steps.title: Identify the contrast
steps.content: The Mornington Railway used broad tracks, while the Rockhampton Steam Tramway used narrow gauge tracks—opposite choices within the "railway gauge muddle."

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
The logical relationship is contrast: the Mornington Railway used broad tracks while the Rockhampton Steam Tramway used narrow gauge tracks — opposite choices that illustrate the "railway gauge muddle." The blank needs a transition signaling that opposition.
  • Choice A — "In sum,": signals a concluding summary, so fails — we are in the middle of giving examples, not wrapping up. ✗
  • Choice B — "By contrast,": directly marks opposition between the two lines' track widths, fitting perfectly. ✓
  • Choice C — "Similarly,": signals parallel sameness, so fails — the two lines used opposite gauges, not the same one. ✗
  • Choice D — "For instance,": signals an illustrative example of a preceding general claim, so fails — the second railway line is a contrasting case, not another example of the same thing. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
